name: Lobos Salvajes desde Peñarroya de Tastavins — comarca del Matarraña
region_intro: Mountain biking around Peñarroya De Tastavins offers diverse terrain within the Matarraña region, characterized by the rugged Puertos de Beceite mountains. The landscape features impressive rock formations like the Masmut Rocks, deep ravines, and extensive pine forests. Riders can explore routes that traverse varied elevations, including trails around the Pena Reservoir. Best mountain bike trails around Peñarroya De Tastavins The most popular mountain bike trail is Lobos Salvajes desde Peñarroya de Tastavins — comarca del Matarraña, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Matarraña region's varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Masmut Rocks Loop Trail, a difficult 34.0 miles (54.7 km) path. This trail offers views of the impressive Masmut Rocks, known for their vertical walls. Local mountain bikers also love the Urocha Inn, Beceite – Pena Reservoir loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins / Pena-roja de Tastavins, a 41.0 miles (66.0 km) trail leading through the area around the Pena Reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Peñarroya De Tastavins is defined by the Puertos de Beceite mountains, deep ravines, and pine forests. The network offers routes catering to various skill levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate paths. The routes in Peñarroya De Tastavins are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Peñarroya De Tastavins's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: Peñarroya de Tastavins offers a wide network of mountain bike trails. On komoot, you'll find 15 routes, ranging from easy to difficult. The region itself boasts over 100 kilometers of marked and approved routes, part of the 'Peñarroya 1300' and 'Sierras Matarranya' networks, catering to all skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: Yes, the region offers trails suitable for various skill levels, including family-friendly excursions. While most routes on komoot are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. The 'Peñarroya 1300' network is designed to include options for less experienced riders, ensuring families can find suitable paths to enjoy the natural surroundings.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: The terrain around Peñarroya de Tastavins is diverse, characterized by the rugged Puertos de Beceite mountains. You'll encounter impressive rock formations, deep ravines, and extensive pine forests. Trails often feature varied elevations, including challenging ascents and descents, as well as paths around the Pena Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Peñarroya de Tastavins are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995908524/masmut-rocks-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masmut Rocks Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.0-mile (54.7 km) path offering views of the impressive Masmut Rocks. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23754295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urocha Inn, Beceite – Pena Reservoir loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins / Pena-roja de Tastavins&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 41.0-mile (66.0 km) trail.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or natural attractions I can see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can ride past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masmut Rocks&lt;/a&gt;, a conglomerate monolith with vertical walls over 100 meters high, known for its vulture colony. The area also features picturesque ravines and extensive pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful 30-meter high waterfall that can be a rewarding stop. Additionally, routes often pass by the spectacular Pena Reservoir.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 30-meter high cascade, is a notable natural feature in the area. While not directly on a specific komoot MTB route listed, its surrounding landscape is beautiful and can be explored as part of the wider network of trails. Other waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pool&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are also in the vicinity.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: The mountain bike trails in Peñarroya de Tastavins are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied landscapes, the challenging ascents, and the opportunity to explore the rugged beauty of the Puertos de Beceite mountains.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Peñarroya de Tastavins is known for its challenging routes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995932143/lobos-salvajes-desde-penarroya-de-tastavins-comarca-del-matarrana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lobos Salvajes desde Peñarroya de Tastavins — comarca del Matarraña&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29.4-mile (47.3 km) trail with over 1,400 meters of elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Masmut Cliffs – Cingla de Sant Jaume loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins / Pena-roja de Tastavins&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, covering 34.2 miles (34.2 km) with significant climbing.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: Yes, the region is well-equipped for mountain bikers. Many routes originate and conclude at a designated 'StartPoint - Peñarroya1300,' which typically includes an information panel and a service station for cyclists. These start points usually offer parking facilities, making it convenient to access the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: The Matarraña region, including Peñarroya de Tastavins, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are generally mild, but it's always wise to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any places to stay or eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Peñarroya de Tastavins and the surrounding Matarraña region offer various accommodation options, from rural guesthouses to hotels. You'll also find local cafes and pubs in the villages where you can refuel after your ride. For specific options, you might look into places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boixar&lt;/a&gt; or explore the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3818402/huts-and-cabins-around-penarroya-de-tastavins" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;huts and cabins&lt;/a&gt; for a more rustic experience.

Q: Can I observe wildlife while mountain biking in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: The natural environment of Peñarroya de Tastavins provides opportunities to observe various wildlife. Keep an eye out for Iberian goats, wild boars, and squirrels. The Masmut Rocks are also home to a vulture colony, and there's a designated feeding area for these birds, offering a chance to observe them in their natural habitat.
tips: Near the town of Peñarroya de Tastavins, along the path that leads to the viewpoint, we find these wonderful views of the Peñas del Masmut that will undoubtedly make us take out our camera to photograph.

The Masmut viewpoint is a popular destination for lovers of nature and outdoor sports as it offers impressive views of the entire Masmut massif. A wonderful place that I recommend for days with good visibility, along the way we will be accompanied by the Masmut rocks and they will make it very picturesque
This is one of the beautiful pictures that we will find while we ride through the Sierra de Escanade, near the town of Peñarroya de Tastavins, the Cingla de Sant Jaume and the Peñas de Masmut.

Nice town of Peñarroya de Tastavins where the Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Fuente and the Hermitage above and below and the Avellaners fountain stand out as places of interest. It is definitely worth a visit.
